← Back to ResistAct

Privacy Policy

Last updated: April 21, 2026

ResistAct ("we", "us", "the site") is a platform that helps people discover and share acts of civic resistance. This policy explains what we collect, why, and what choices you have.

What we collect

  • Account information — when you sign in with Google or Facebook, we receive your name, email, and profile picture from that provider. We do not receive your password.
  • Activity on ResistAct — acts or facts you submit, boost, or bookmark, and any content you post.
  • Basic technical data — standard server logs (IP address, timestamps, browser type) used for security and debugging.

How we use it

  • To show you content, save your boosts and bookmarks, and let you post.
  • To moderate the community and enforce our Terms.
  • To keep the site secure and to fix bugs.

We do not sell your personal data. We do not use it for third-party advertising.

Who we share it with

  • Supabase — our backend provider, hosts the database and authentication.
  • Google and Meta (Facebook) — only when you sign in via their OAuth; they give us the data listed above.
  • Legal requirements — we will disclose data if compelled by valid legal process.

Retention

We retain your account data until you delete your account. Server logs are kept for up to 90 days.

Your rights

You can request a copy of your data, correct it, or delete it. See our Data Deletion page for the fastest path, or email lnmezz@gmail.com.

Children

ResistAct is not directed at children under 13 and we do not knowingly collect data from them.

Changes

We'll update this page when our practices change. Material changes will be announced on the site.

Contact

Questions? Email lnmezz@gmail.com.
